ogTitle: Wittenoom Hills vs Wogolin: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wittenoom Hills and Wogolin.
heading: Wittenoom Hills vs Wogolin — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wittenoom Hills, Western Australia and Wogolin, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wittenoom Hills has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Wogolin (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wittenoom Hills or Wogolin safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wittenoom Hills has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Wogolin scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wittenoom Hills scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Wogolin scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wittenoom Hills or Wogolin?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wittenoom Hills has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
